content:
Jian Zhou (周健) You can call me Jack.
I am an independent researcher based in Shanghai, China. My research lies at the intersection of number theory, mathematical physics, and condensed matter theory.
Professional Life By day, I work as Director of Product and Game Producer at XD.INC (HKEX: 02400), a Hong Kong-listed gaming company. I lead cross-platform game development teams working with Unreal Engine 5.
Berlin Years (2017–2020) Research Motivation The fine-structure constant α has long fascinated me — this mysterious dimensionless number that Feynman himself called “one of the greatest damn mysteries of physics.” I have been driven by a simple question: can this constant admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression?
This pursuit has led me from α to the Riemann Hypothesis, from superconductivity to membrane models of particles, all connected through the deep arithmetic structure of the Bost-Connes system.
JZ Institute of Science I am the Founder & Director of JZ Institute of Science (JZIS) — a non-profit research organization whose mission is to leverage AI to empower independent researchers and accelerate scientific discovery. JZIS develops open-source tools and frameworks for AI-augmented research, making frontier science more accessible to researchers outside traditional academic institutions.
Contact Email: jack@jzis.org ORCID: 0009-0000-3536-9500 GitHub: JackZH26 Figshare: Jian Zhou X (Twitter): @jackzhj JZIS: jzis.org

Professional Life

By day, I work as Director of Product and Game Producer at XD.INC (HKEX: 02400), a Hong Kong-listed gaming company. I lead cross-platform game development teams working with Unreal Engine 5.

Berlin Years (2017–2020)

Research Motivation

The fine-structure constant α has long fascinated me — this mysterious dimensionless number that Feynman himself called “one of the greatest damn mysteries of physics.” I have been driven by a simple question: can this constant admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression?

title: Publications

content:
Research Directions 1. The Fine-Structure Constant α Central Question: Can the fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137.036 admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression rooted in number theory?
Key Result (2026): The three-term formula
α⁻¹ = 360/φ² − 2/φ³ + 1/(3φ)⁵ = 137.035999165…
agrees with CODATA 2022 to 0.58σ — within experimental uncertainty. All three coefficients are intrinsic arithmetic invariants of the real quadratic field K = ℚ(√5).
The arithmetic framework (Bost-Connes spectral theory):
Term 1 — Spectral invariant: 360/φ² = 3!/ζ_K(−3) · ε_K⁻², where ζ_K(−3) = 1/60 = 1/|A₅| Term 2 — Unit correction: −2/φ³ = −w_K · ε_K⁻³ (torsion of O_K^×) Term 3 — Prime correction: 1/(3φ)⁵ = [Tr(ε_K²) · ε_K]^{−d_K} (resonant inert prime) φ is uniquely selected by the Bose–Einstein self-reference equation n_BE(ln x) = x. The formula has zero free parameters — all quantities are determined by the arithmetic of ℚ(√5).
📄 Papers:
Spectral Invariants of BC_K and Arithmetic Expansion of α⁻¹ — submitted to Experimental Mathematics On the Arithmetic of ℚ(√5) and the Fine-Structure Constant — submitted to Ramanujan Journal (under appeal) ⚙️ Status: Two papers submitted; active research on rigorous derivation of spectral-action framework
2. Spherical Membrane Model of Particles Idea: All quantum fields are vibration modes of S², distinguished by fiber bundle type.
Electrons (Paper I): Bound-state electrons as standing-wave patterns on concentric spherical membranes, with quantum numbers (n, ℓ, m) as vibrational modes. Free electrons emerge as single membranes with continuous spectra.
Photons (Paper II): Photons as expanding spherical membranes S²(t) with R(t) = ct, whose vibration modes are the vector spherical harmonics. Eight features of classical and quantum optics emerge naturally as geometric properties.
Unified fiber bundle classification:
Spin 0 → Scalar membrane (Higgs) Spin 1/2 → Spinor membrane (electrons) Spin 1 → Vector membrane (photons) Spin 2 → Tensor membrane (gravitons) ⚙️ Status: Electron membrane paper submitted to Foundations of Physics
3. Room-Temperature Superconductivity Approach: Two-channel Allen-Dynes framework combining phonon and spin-fluctuation coupling with quantum-metric superfluid stiffness.
Key Results:
Blind predictions for 19 materials achieve R²log = 0.96 across five orders of magnitude in Tc No-go theorem: quantum metric cannot directly modify electron-phonon coupling in single-band, isotropic superconductors 7 candidate materials with predicted Tc > 200 K identified 📄 Preprint: Two-Channel Allen-Dynes Framework
⚙️ Status: Under review at Superconductor Science and Technology
📊 Overall: 20 preprints on Figshare, 4 on arXiv, 600+ views, 450+ downloads.
